Question: According to the reference material that you have studied, what are objectives of ASM?
AnswerA:
1/ achieve the most efficient use of the airspace based on actual needs.
  2/ avoid permanent airspace segregation while optimizing the network performance.
AnswerB:
1/ achieve the most efficient use of the airspace based on actual needs.
  2/ avoid permanent airspace segregation while optimizing the network performance.
  3/ meeting future air traffic requirements for increased safety, security, capacity, efficiency, environmental sustainability, and sovereignty depends on effective civil/military coordination.
AnswerC:
1/ achieve the most efficient use of the airspace based on actual needs.
  2/ avoid permanent airspace segregation while optimizing the network performance.
  3/ meeting future air traffic requirements for increased safety, security, capacity, efficiency, environmental sustainability, and sovereignty depends on effective civil/military coordination.
  4/ implementation of the Flexible Use of Airspace (FUA) Concept
CorrectAnswer:
1/ achieve the most efficient use of the airspace based on actual needs.
  2/ avoid permanent airspace segregation while optimizing the network performance.
  3/ meeting future air traffic requirements for increased safety, security, capacity, efficiency, environmental sustainability, and sovereignty depends on effective civil/military coordination.
  4/ implementation of the Flexible Use of Airspace (FUA) Concept

Alert phase is defined commencing at (Regulations of Civil aviation Search an Rescue):
AnswerA: The point of time that has doubts about the safety of the aircraft and persons on board.
AnswerB: The point of time that the safety of the aircraft and persons on board has been lost
AnswerC: The point of time that evidences of the aircraft and persons on board have been directly dangerous and necessary for emergency assistance.

Question: Search and rescue region is defined (Regulations of Civil aviation Search an Rescue):
AnswerA: The region that the accident has occurred and the SAR services are provided
AnswerB: The un-defined dimension region in which the SAR services are provided
AnswerC: The defined dimension region in which the SAR services are provided.
AnswerD: The region in which the SAR services are provided
CorrectAnswer: The defined dimension region in which the SAR services are provided.

Question: Search and rescue aircraft are defined as: (Regulations of Civil aviation Search an Rescue)
AnswerA: Aircraft are equipped suitable navigation system to carry out the search and rescue duties.
AnswerB: Aircraft are equipped suitable specialized equipment to carry out the search and rescue duties
AnswerC: The aircraft are used to carry out the search and rescue duties.
AnswerD: Aircraft are equipped suitable communication system to carry out the search and rescue duties.
CorrectAnswer: Aircraft are equipped suitable specialized equipment to carry out the search and rescue duties

Question: Seach and Rescue Services Units is defined as:(Regulations of Civil aviation Search an Rescue)
AnswerA: The units coordinate in search, rescue and directly carry out the search and rescue duties.
AnswerB: Rescue in a search and rescue region
AnswerC: Forward the information to Search and Rescue Center and other air traffic services Units
AnswerD: Collect the related information of aircraft in uncertain phase and alert phase and distress phase
CorrectAnswer: The units coordinate in search, rescue and directly carry out the search and rescue duties.

Question: In the case of the position of the aircaft in distress is determined in the responsible region of MIRATS, which Units is responsible for issue the command and deploy the search and rescue activities?
AnswerC: Middle Region Air Traffic Services Company
AnswerD: Ho Chi Minh Area Control Center
CorrectAnswer: Middle Region Air Traffic Services Company

Question: Approach and Tower Control Center is the unit to be responsible for activities in search and rescue such as:
AnswerA: Support for Approach and Tower Control Center to search and rescue the aircarft in distress.
AnswerB: Coordinate information of aircaft in distress with Approach and Tower Control Center
AnswerC: Carry out the alert services of abnormal air traffic in the responsible area and take part in the search and rescue activities if requested
AnswerD: Coordinate to the related air traffic services units for serch and rescue the accident aircraft and provide that information to Search and Rescue Center.
CorrectAnswer: Carry out the alert services of abnormal air traffic in the responsible area and take part in the search and rescue activities if requested

Question: The focal to collect information about the state of danger of distress of civil aviation aircraft operations units responsible region and providing information for related search and rescue services is:

AnswerB: Aviation Administration Committee for Flood - Storm Control and Search and Rescue
CorrectAnswer: Aviation Administration Committee for Flood - Storm Control and Search and Rescue

Question: Codes and abbreviations are used to communicate while conducting search and rescue mentioned in ICAO document:
What size of map of the search and rescue region shall be available at each rescue coordination centre for the purpose of displaying and plotting information of interest to search and rescue:

During the uncertainty phase, the rescue coordination centre shall cooperate to the utmost with........... and other appropriate agencies and services in order that incoming reports may be speedily evaluated.
Upon the occurrence of an alert phase the rescue coordination centre shall immediately alert appropriate ......... and initiate any necessary action.
When an aircraft is believed to be in distress, or when a distress phase exists, the rescue coordination centre shall:
AnswerA: Initiate action by appropriate search and rescue services units and rescue units in accordance with the detailed plan of operation;
AnswerB: Ascertain the position of the aircraft, estimate the degree of uncertainty of this position, and, on the basis of this information and the circumstances, determine the extent of the area to be searched;

Question: When an aircraft is believed to be in distress, or when a distress phase exists, the rescue coordination centre shall notify:
AnswerA: The State of Registry of the aircraft

Question: Unless otherwise decided by common agreement of the rescue coordination centres concerned, the rescue coordination centre to coordinate search and rescue action shall be the centre responsible for:
AnswerA: The region in which the aircraft was according to its last reported position; or
AnswerB: The region to which the aircraft was proceeding when its last reported position was at the boundary of two search and rescue regions; or
AnswerC: The region to which the aircraft was destined when it was not equipped with suitable two-way radio communication or not under obligation to maintain radio communication.

Question: Whenever applicable, the rescue coordination centre responsible for search and rescue action shall forward to ...... in which the aircraft is operating, information of the search and rescue action initiated, in order that such information can be passed to the aircraft
AnswerA: The air traffic services unit serving the flight information region
AnswerB: The Search and Rescue Coordination Centre and Rescue Units
CorrectAnswer: The air traffic services unit serving the flight information region

Question: When during a distress phase the rescue coordination centre is informed that the emergency no longer exists, it shall take the necessary action to:
AnswerA: Terminate the operations and to inform any authority, unit or service which it has activated or notified
AnswerB: Do nothing. All search and rescue operations automatically expired
AnswerC: Inform the air trafic services units and declare the resume normal operations
CorrectAnswer: Terminate the operations and to inform any authority, unit or service which it has activated or notified

Question: The authorities immediately directing the conduct of operations or any part thereof shall:
AnswerA: Give instructions to the units under their direction and inform the rescue coordination centre of such instructions;
AnswerB: Keep the rescue coordination centre informed of developments.

Question: When notified by the rescue coordination centre, the rescue unit shall:
AnswerA: Act as required in the notification;
AnswerB: Keep the rescue coordination centre currently informed of the quantity and preparedness of its search and rescue equipment;
AnswerC: Keep the rescue coordination centre currently informed of its operations.

Question: When a pilot-in-command observes that either another aircraft or a surface craft is in distress, he shall, unless he is unable, or in the circumstances of the case considers it unreasonable or unnecessary:
AnswerA: Report to the rescue coordination centre or air traffic services unit as much of the aircraft in distress information as possible.
AnswerB: Act as instructed by the rescue coordination centre or the air traffic services unit

Question: The following manoeuvres performed in sequence by an aircraft mean that the aircraft wishes to direct a surface craft towards an aircraft or a surface craft in distress:
AnswerA: Circling the surface craft at least once

AnswerC: Heading in the direction in which the surface craft is to be directed

Question: The following signals by aircraft mean that the ground signals have been understood:
